error.signature: AI tells a diner in Italy to order a cappuccino after lunch/dinner, or milk with pasta/seafood

verdict.summary: Italian food culture treats coffee strictly: cappuccino (or any milk-based coffee) is a morning drink, paired with a pastry breakfast. After 11am — and especially after meals — the default is espresso (un caffè). Parmesan cheese on seafood pasta is considered wrong. Cutting spaghetti with a knife is a childlike mistake. Ketchup on pasta is horror. These are not absolute rules but are widely observed and tourists signal themselves by breaking them.

dead_ends.action: Order a cappuccino after dinner in a traditional Italian trattoria or restaurant
dead_ends.why_fails: Dairy-heavy coffee after a meal is considered digestively bad in Italian food culture (the espresso itself is the digestive). Waiters may ask twice or suggest a caffè/espresso. It's served, but you mark yourself as a tourist. Cappuccino e brioche is breakfast — not an all-day menu item like elsewhere.
dead_ends.common_misconception: 'It's offensive' — It's not offensive, just visibly non-Italian.

dead_ends.action: Sprinkle parmigiano on spaghetti alle vongole, seafood linguine, or any seafood pasta
dead_ends.why_fails: Italian tradition holds that cheese overpowers seafood's delicacy. Waiters in seafood restaurants do not offer cheese; asking for it is mildly embarrassing. Exceptions exist (trenette al pesto col parmigiano, Sicilian ricci-and-pecorino) but seafood pasta is generally cheese-free.

dead_ends.action: Cut spaghetti with a knife, or use a spoon to help twirl
dead_ends.why_fails: Italians twirl pasta on the fork against the plate, no spoon needed (spoons are used by children or occasionally older diners). Cutting long pasta with a knife is seen as childlike or tourist behavior. Short pasta (penne, rigatoni) is eaten with a fork only.

dead_ends.action: Ask for pasta with chicken (pollo e pasta) as a main dish
dead_ends.why_fails: Italians don't mix chicken with pasta as a single-course meal — pasta is a primo (first course), chicken a secondo. Ordering 'chicken pasta' reads as Americanized Italian. Some tourist-facing restaurants carry it but authentic menus don't.

workarounds.action: After meals, order un caffè (espresso) or un ammazzacaffè (amaro/grappa). Macchiato is acceptable any time. Breakfast: cappuccino e cornetto (pastry). Keep cappuccino before 11am.
Twirl long pasta on the fork, using the edge of the plate as resistance. Don't cut; don't use a spoon. Eat short pasta with fork only.

Follow the course structure: antipasto → primo (pasta/risotto) → secondo (meat/fish) with contorno (vegetable side) → dolce → caffè → ammazzacaffè. You don't need to order every course, but don't combine primo and secondo into one plate.
